these differ in different countries, we come across a great variety of
intellectual phenomena which otherwise would be simply inexplicable.
Europeans living in tropical climates exhibit a change in their intellect
in the short space of two or three generations. Negroes in temperat.e
Zones do the same and we can quote examples, wherefu the brain
of man, by change in food, clothing and climate has undergone
material alterations, in some cases showing sigas of clear deterio
ration and in others perceptible progression. How 'are we t.o
account for this strange phenomena in the human intellect !
Are we to suppose that these phenomena are not effected by
the light and heat we get from the Sun ? How · can we
account for these changes, if not by the influences from the Sun.?
